import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut i = 0
  mut current = 0
  mut has = false
  mut neg = false
  while i <= n
    let b = if i < n
      match string.byte_at(s, i)
      when Some(x)
        x
      when None
        32
      end
    else
      32
    end
    if b >= 48 and b <= 57
      current = current * 10 + (b - 48)
      has = true
    else if b == 45 and not has
      neg = true
    else
      if has
        if neg
          result = list.push(result, -current)
        else
          result = list.push(result, current)
        end
        current = 0
        has = false
        neg = false
      else
        neg = false
      end
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  result
end

fn bsearch(_ xs: List<Int>, _ t: Int) -> Option<Int>
  mut lo = 0
  mut hi = xs.len()
  while lo < hi
    let mid = (lo + hi) / 2
    let v = match xs.get(mid)
    when Some(x)
      x
    when None
      0
    end
    if v == t
      return Some(mid)
    else if v < t
      lo = mid + 1
    else
      hi = mid
    end
  end
  None
end

fn main() -> Unit
  let line1 = match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    s
  when None
    ""
  end
  let line2 = match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    s
  when None
    ""
  end
  let xs = parse_ints(line1)
  let tlist = parse_ints(line2)
  let t = match tlist.get(0)
  when Some(x)
    x
  when None
    0
  end
  match bsearch(xs, t)
  when Some(i)
    print("index=#{i}")
  when None
    print("not_found")
  end
end
